title: Dragon Idea Engine — Master Codex
issueTitle: Codex: Dragon Idea Engine — Master Codex
summary:
Dragon Idea Engine is an **autonomous software factory** designed to turn ideas into fully developed software projects using modular AI agents.
The system:
```
Idea → Architecture → Repo → Issues → Code → Review → Deploy → Feedback
```
Agents operate through a **plugin-based runtime** and communicate through an asynchronous message queue.
Event-driven agent systems commonly use a queue broker so independent workers can process tasks asynchronously and scale horizontally.
body:
# Codex: Dragon Idea Engine — Master Codex

Source section: `codex/sections/01-dragon-idea-engine-master-codex.md`

## Summary

Dragon Idea Engine is an **autonomous software factory** designed to turn ideas into fully developed software projects using modular AI agents.
The system:
```
Idea → Architecture → Repo → Issues → Code → Review → Deploy → Feedback
```
Agents operate through a **plugin-based runtime** and communicate through an asynchronous message queue.
Event-driven agent systems commonly use a queue broker so independent workers can process tasks asynchronously and scale horizontally.

## Scope Checklist

- [ ] Root Repo
- [ ] Idea Agent
- [ ] Architect Agent
- [ ] Repository Manager Agent
- [ ] Issue Generator Agent
- [ ] Developer Agent
- [ ] Review Agent
- [ ] Test Agent
- [ ] Refactor Agent
- [ ] Documentation Agent
- [ ] Feedback Agent
- [ ] Ideas
- [ ] Projects
- [ ] Jobs
- [ ] Agents
- [ ] Credentials

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] The implementation plan for this codex section is documented.
- [ ] Dependencies or sequencing constraints are identified.
- [ ] Follow-up engineering tasks are clear enough to execute.

title: Dragon Idea Engine — Master Codex Addendum
issueTitle: Codex: Dragon Idea Engine — Master Codex Addendum
summary:
Define the **standard contract for agent jobs** across the entire system.
All agents must consume and produce jobs using this schema.
This ensures:
```
compatibility
traceability
retry support
debugging
orchestration
```

title: Dragon Idea Engine — Infrastructure Architecture
issueTitle: Codex: Dragon Idea Engine — Infrastructure Architecture
summary:
This section defines the **deployment model and repository layout** for the Dragon Idea Engine platform.
The system is designed to operate in two phases:
```
Phase 1: Single-node Raspberry Pi Engine
Phase 2: Distributed Raspberry Pi Cluster
```
The **single-node system must always remain fully functional** without requiring cloud services.
# Deployment Philosophy
Dragon Idea Engine prioritizes:
```
self-hosted
portable
low-power infrastructure
edge deployment
distributed scalability
```
Primary target platform:
```
Raspberry Pi 5
```
Recommended configuration:
```
8GB RAM
NVMe SSD via PCIe
Ubuntu Server
Docker
```
# Phase 1 — Raspberry Pi Autonomous Engine
Phase 1 runs **all components on a single device**.
Architecture:
```
                ┌───────────────────────┐
                │ Dragon UI (React)     │
                └─────────────┬─────────┘
                              │
                              ▼
                      ┌───────────────┐
                      │ Dragon API    │
                      └───────┬───────┘
                              │
                              ▼
                      ┌───────────────┐
                      │ Orchestrator  │
                      └───────┬───────┘
                              │
                              ▼
                        RabbitMQ Queue
                              │
                              ▼
                     Agent Runner Workers
                              │
                              ▼
                        Agent Plugins
```
Everything runs locally.
# Containers on the Pi
The Pi runs a Docker stack containing:
```
dragon-ui
dragon-api
dragon-orchestrator
dragon-agent-runner
rabbitmq
postgres
```
Optional:
```
ollama (local AI models)
redis (cache)
```
# Local AI Support
Dragon Idea Engine should support both:
```
cloud AI providers
local models
```
Local inference options:
```
Ollama
Local Llama models
Mistral
Code Llama
```
This allows:
```
offline operation
lower cost
privacy
```
# Raspberry Pi Image
The project will eventually produce a **Dragon Idea Engine OS image**.
Image includes:
```
Ubuntu Server
Docker
RabbitMQ
Postgres
Dragon Engine services
Agent Runner
React UI
```
Boot experience:
```
power on
engine starts
UI accessible on LAN
```
Example:
```
http://dragon.local
```
# Storage Layout
Recommended filesystem structure:
```
/dragon-engine
    /config
    /repos
    /workspaces
    /logs
    /agents
    /models
```
Details:
```
repos       → cached repositories
workspaces  → temporary agent workspaces
logs        → job and system logs
agents      → plugin agents
models      → local AI models
```
# Workspace Model
Developer agents create temporary workspaces.
Example:
```
/workspaces
    dragon-crm
        issue-42
        issue-43
```
Workflow:
```
clone repo
implement issue
push PR
destroy workspace
```
This ensures:
```
parallel agents
clean builds
safe execution
```
# Phase 2 — Raspberry Pi Cluster
Phase 2 introduces **horizontal scaling** using multiple Pi nodes.
Cluster model:
```
                ┌───────────────────┐
                │ Control Node Pi   │
                │ Dragon Engine     │
                └──────────┬────────┘
                           │
                      RabbitMQ
                           │
        ┌───────────────┬───────────────┬───────────────┐
        │ Worker Pi     │ Worker Pi     │ Worker Pi     │
        │ Agent Runner  │ Agent Runner  │ Agent Runner  │
        └───────────────┴───────────────┴───────────────┘
```
# Cluster Technology
Recommended orchestrator:
```
Docker Swarm
```
Reasons:
```
simple setup
lightweight
native Docker support
works well on ARM
```
Alternative options:
```
K3s (lightweight Kubernetes)
Nomad
```
# Cluster Responsibilities
### Control Node
Runs:
```
dragon-ui
dragon-api
orchestrator
rabbitmq
postgres
```
### Worker Nodes
Run:
```
dragon-agent-runner
agent plugins
```
Workers consume jobs from RabbitMQ.
# Scaling Model
Example cluster:
```
1 control node
5 worker nodes
```
Possible concurrent agents:
```
50+ jobs
```
Because:
```
each Pi runs multiple containers
```
# Job Scheduling
RabbitMQ distributes jobs across workers.
Agents become:
```
stateless workers
```
Advantages:
```
fault tolerance
easy scaling
high throughput
```
# Repository Storage Strategy
Repositories may be stored on:
```
local disk
shared NFS
distributed storage
```
Recommended:
```
control node repo cache
worker temporary clones
```
# Network Requirements
All nodes must share a local network.
Example:
```
1Gb Ethernet
```
Recommended topology:
```
Gigabit switch
wired network
```
# Cluster Expansion
Adding nodes should be simple.
Example:
```
flash Dragon Pi image
connect to network
join swarm
```
New worker automatically begins consuming jobs.
# Edge + Cloud Hybrid (Optional)
Future architecture may support hybrid operation.
Example:
```
local cluster
+
cloud GPU agents
```
Possible uses:
```
heavy AI workloads
large model inference
code analysis
```
But the system must **never require cloud infrastructure** to function.
# Repository Layout (Full Project)
Final recommended GitHub structure for the main repository:
```
DragonIdeaEngine
│
├─ docs
│   MASTER_CODEX.md
│   AGENT_JOB_SCHEMA.md
│   AGENT_PLUGIN_SPEC.md
│   AGENT_LIFECYCLE.md
│   ARCHITECTURE.md
│
├─ runner
│   dragon-agent-runner
│
├─ sdk
│   dragon-agent-sdk
│
├─ agents
│   architect-agent
│   developer-agent
│   review-agent
│   test-agent
│   refactor-agent
│   documentation-agent
│   feedback-agent
│
├─ services
│   dragon-api
│   dragon-orchestrator
│
├─ ui
│   dragon-ui
│
├─ infrastructure
│   docker
│   swarm
│   raspberrypi-image
│
├─ templates
│   project-templates
│
└─ examples
    sample-project
```
# Long Term Vision
Dragon Idea Engine becomes:
```
a personal autonomous development lab
a distributed Pi software factory
an extensible AI agent ecosystem
```
Running on a small cluster of Raspberry Pis, it can:
```
generate new applications
maintain repositories
process user feedback
evolve software continuously
```
All from a **self-hosted system sitting on a desk or rack**.
body:
# Codex: Dragon Idea Engine — Infrastructure Architecture

Source section: `codex/sections/03-dragon-idea-engine-infrastructure-architecture.md`

## Summary

This section defines the **deployment model and repository layout** for the Dragon Idea Engine platform.
The system is designed to operate in two phases:
```
Phase 1: Single-node Raspberry Pi Engine
Phase 2: Distributed Raspberry Pi Cluster
```
The **single-node system must always remain fully functional** without requiring cloud services.
# Deployment Philosophy
Dragon Idea Engine prioritizes:
```
self-hosted
portable
low-power infrastructure
edge deployment
distributed scalability
```

```
Raspberry Pi 5

Recommended configuration:
```
8GB RAM
NVMe SSD via PCIe
Ubuntu Server
Docker
```

Phase 1 runs **all components on a single device**.

```
                ┌───────────────────────┐

                              ▼

                        RabbitMQ Queue

Everything runs locally.
# Containers on the Pi

title: IDEA_SCORING_AND_SELECTION_SYSTEM
summary:
Dragon Idea Engine receives ideas from multiple sources. Not all ideas should become projects.
The Idea Scoring System evaluates each idea and determines whether the system should:
```
build
defer
reject
archive
```
This prevents uncontrolled repository growth and focuses development resources on high-value opportunities.
body:
# Codex: IDEA_SCORING_AND_SELECTION_SYSTEM

Source section: `codex/sections/04-idea-scoring-and-selection-system.md`

## Summary

Dragon Idea Engine receives ideas from multiple sources. Not all ideas should become projects.
The Idea Scoring System evaluates each idea and determines whether the system should:
```
build
defer
reject
archive
```
This prevents uncontrolled repository growth and focuses development resources on high-value opportunities.

## Scope Checklist

- [ ] Market Demand
- [ ] Technical Feasibility
- [ ] Development Cost
- [ ] Differentiation
- [ ] Monetization Potential
- [ ] Strategic Value
- [ ] Idea Queue
- [ ] Idea Scores
- [ ] Idea Voting
- [ ] Recommended Next Codex Section

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] The implementation plan for this codex section is documented.
- [ ] Dependencies or sequencing constraints are identified.
- [ ] Follow-up engineering tasks are clear enough to execute.

title: RISK_ANALYSIS_AND_COMPLIANCE_SCORING
summary:
Before an idea can be approved for development, Dragon Idea Engine must evaluate **legal, regulatory, and ethical risks** associated with the project.
This ensures the system does not automatically generate projects that expose the operator to legal liability.
The Risk Analysis system reduces the likelihood of building projects that involve:
```
privacy violations
regulated industries
copyright infringement
data misuse
platform abuse
```
Risk analysis contributes a **negative weighting factor** in the idea scoring system.
body:
# Codex: RISK_ANALYSIS_AND_COMPLIANCE_SCORING

Source section: `codex/sections/05-risk-analysis-and-compliance-scoring.md`

## Summary

Before an idea can be approved for development, Dragon Idea Engine must evaluate **legal, regulatory, and ethical risks** associated with the project.
This ensures the system does not automatically generate projects that expose the operator to legal liability.
The Risk Analysis system reduces the likelihood of building projects that involve:
```
privacy violations
regulated industries
copyright infringement
data misuse
platform abuse
```
Risk analysis contributes a **negative weighting factor** in the idea scoring system.

## Scope Checklist

- [ ] Privacy Risk
- [ ] Recommended Next Codex Section

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] The implementation plan for this codex section is documented.
- [ ] Dependencies or sequencing constraints are identified.
- [ ] Follow-up engineering tasks are clear enough to execute.

title: ETHICS_ANALYSIS_AGENT
summary:
The **Ethics Analysis Agent** evaluates whether a proposed project could cause **harm, misuse, manipulation, or social risk**.
Legal compliance alone is not sufficient. Many harmful systems are technically legal but ethically questionable.
Examples include:
```
mass persuasion systems
manipulative addiction mechanics
surveillance tools
exploitative gig-economy automation
deepfake generation tools
```
The Ethics Agent ensures Dragon Idea Engine avoids building harmful technologies.
body:
# Codex: ETHICS_ANALYSIS_AGENT

Source section: `codex/sections/06-ethics-analysis-agent.md`

## Summary

The **Ethics Analysis Agent** evaluates whether a proposed project could cause **harm, misuse, manipulation, or social risk**.
Legal compliance alone is not sufficient. Many harmful systems are technically legal but ethically questionable.
Examples include:
```
mass persuasion systems
manipulative addiction mechanics
surveillance tools
exploitative gig-economy automation
deepfake generation tools
```
The Ethics Agent ensures Dragon Idea Engine avoids building harmful technologies.

## Scope Checklist

- [ ] Harm Potential
- [ ] Manipulation Risk
- [ ] Privacy Intrusion
- [ ] Social Impact
- [ ] Misuse Potential

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] The implementation plan for this codex section is documented.
- [ ] Dependencies or sequencing constraints are identified.
- [ ] Follow-up engineering tasks are clear enough to execute.

title: REUSABLE_COMPONENT_LIBRARY
summary:
Dragon Idea Engine should avoid repeatedly generating the same infrastructure code across projects.
Instead, it maintains a **Reusable Component Library** consisting of standardized modules and shared services.
Benefits:
```
faster project generation
consistent architecture
reduced bugs
centralized security updates
smaller codebases
```
All generated projects should reference reusable components whenever possible.
subheadings: One more codex section you will likely want next
body:
# Codex: REUSABLE_COMPONENT_LIBRARY

Source section: `codex/sections/07-reusable-component-library.md`

## Summary

Dragon Idea Engine should avoid repeatedly generating the same infrastructure code across projects.
Instead, it maintains a **Reusable Component Library** consisting of standardized modules and shared services.
Benefits:
```
faster project generation
consistent architecture
reduced bugs
centralized security updates
smaller codebases
```
All generated projects should reference reusable components whenever possible.

## Scope Checklist

- [ ] One more codex section you will likely want next

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] The implementation plan for this codex section is documented.
- [ ] Dependencies or sequencing constraints are identified.
- [ ] Follow-up engineering tasks are clear enough to execute.

title: AGENT_MEMORY_AND_KNOWLEDGE_SYSTEM
summary:
The Agent Memory and Knowledge System allows Dragon Idea Engine to retain knowledge across project lifecycles. Instead of treating each project as an isolated event, the platform accumulates operational, architectural, and market knowledge.
This enables the system to:
``` id="mem-1"
learn from successful projects
avoid repeating failed designs
reuse proven architectural patterns
improve idea scoring accuracy
recommend reliable components
```
The goal is for the platform to become progressively more effective over time.
body:
# Codex: AGENT_MEMORY_AND_KNOWLEDGE_SYSTEM

Source section: `codex/sections/08-agent-memory-and-knowledge-system.md`

## Summary

The Agent Memory and Knowledge System allows Dragon Idea Engine to retain knowledge across project lifecycles. Instead of treating each project as an isolated event, the platform accumulates operational, architectural, and market knowledge.
This enables the system to:
``` id="mem-1"
learn from successful projects
avoid repeating failed designs
reuse proven architectural patterns
improve idea scoring accuracy
recommend reliable components
```
The goal is for the platform to become progressively more effective over time.

- [ ] Dependencies or sequencing constraints are identified.
- [ ] Follow-up engineering tasks are clear enough to execute.

title: MANDATORY_OPEN_SOURCE_POLICY
summary:
All code generated by Dragon Idea Engine, whether for internal testing, projects, or user-submitted ideas, **must be open source**. This ensures:
``` id="os-1"
transparency
community collaboration
legal clarity
reusability
security auditability
```
No project should remain closed source unless explicitly approved under exceptional circumstances with human oversight.

All code generated by Dragon Idea Engine, whether for internal testing, projects, or user-submitted ideas, **must be open source**. This ensures:

title: PLATFORM_DISTRIBUTION_AND_COLLABORATION_MODEL
summary:
## Status
``` id="dist-0"
EXPERIMENTAL
```
This section intentionally avoids defining licensing or ownership policies until the long-term governance model is finalized.
The current focus is on **technical architecture and ecosystem collaboration**, not legal structure.
# Platform Distribution Vision
Dragon Idea Engine is designed to eventually operate as a **distributed innovation platform** where multiple users and systems can participate.
Possible participation models may include:
``` id="dist-1"
running local agent nodes
contributing ideas
testing generated projects
improving reusable components
providing research data
```
However, the exact **rights, ownership, and licensing structure** remains undecided.
# Node Participation Concept
The platform may eventually support distributed compute participation.
Participants could run **Agent Nodes** on their own infrastructure.
Example environments include:
``` id="dist-2"
Raspberry Pi systems
local servers
cloud VMs
developer workstations
```
These nodes may assist with:
``` id="dist-3"
idea analysis
code generation
testing
media generation
research tasks
```
Final participation rules will be defined later.
# Idea Contribution Concept
The system may allow external users to submit ideas to the platform.
Ideas could be:
``` id="dist-4"
personal project ideas
startup concepts
automation tools
hardware prototypes
research experiments
```
Accepted ideas may trigger automated development workflows.
The future platform may support:
``` id="dist-5"
public idea pools
private idea submissions
collaborative development
```
No ownership rules are currently defined.
# Hardware Distribution Possibilities
Dragon Idea Engine may eventually be distributed as **preconfigured hardware systems**.
Potential formats include:
``` id="dist-6"
Raspberry Pi single-node kits
Raspberry Pi cluster kits
prebuilt server appliances
virtual machine images
container bundles
```
These would allow users to run the platform locally with minimal setup.
Implementation details will be determined later.
# Ecosystem Goals
The long-term platform aims to encourage:
``` id="dist-7"
collaborative innovation
distributed compute participation
shared reusable components
rapid product experimentation
```
However, governance, licensing, and commercial structures will be addressed in a future codex revision.
# Temporary Rule
Until governance decisions are finalized:
``` id="dist-8"
focus on architecture
focus on technical capabilities
avoid embedding licensing restrictions into generated systems
```
Agents should not assume any licensing model when generating projects.
# Future Section Placeholder
A future codex revision will define:
``` id="dist-9"
platform licensing
contribution requirements
idea ownership
commercial rights
revenue models
```
This section will eventually become:
``` id="dist-10"
PLATFORM_GOVERNANCE_AND_LICENSE_MODEL
```
### Recommended Next Codex Section
Now that you’ve defined:
- idea scoring
- risk analysis
- ethics agent
- reusable component library
- knowledge system
- distributed nodes
…the **next extremely useful section** would be:
**AGENT ORCHESTRATION ENGINE**
This defines **how agents actually coordinate tasks**, queue work, and collaborate through services like:
-
-
Right now the codex defines *what agents exist*, but not **how they coordinate work**, which is the heart of the system.
body:
# Codex: PLATFORM_DISTRIBUTION_AND_COLLABORATION_MODEL

Source section: `codex/sections/10-platform-distribution-and-collaboration-model.md`

## Summary

## Status
``` id="dist-0"
EXPERIMENTAL
```
This section intentionally avoids defining licensing or ownership policies until the long-term governance model is finalized.
The current focus is on **technical architecture and ecosystem collaboration**, not legal structure.
# Platform Distribution Vision
Dragon Idea Engine is designed to eventually operate as a **distributed innovation platform** where multiple users and systems can participate.
Possible participation models may include:
``` id="dist-1"
running local agent nodes
contributing ideas
testing generated projects
improving reusable components
providing research data
```
However, the exact **rights, ownership, and licensing structure** remains undecided.
# Node Participation Concept
The platform may eventually support distributed compute participation.
Participants could run **Agent Nodes** on their own infrastructure.
Example environments include:
``` id="dist-2"
Raspberry Pi systems
local servers
cloud VMs
developer workstations
```
These nodes may assist with:
``` id="dist-3"
idea analysis
code generation
testing
media generation
research tasks
```
Final participation rules will be defined later.
# Idea Contribution Concept
The system may allow external users to submit ideas to the platform.
Ideas could be:
``` id="dist-4"
personal project ideas
startup concepts
automation tools
hardware prototypes
research experiments
```
Accepted ideas may trigger automated development workflows.
The future platform may support:
``` id="dist-5"
public idea pools
private idea submissions
collaborative development
```
No ownership rules are currently defined.
# Hardware Distribution Possibilities
Dragon Idea Engine may eventually be distributed as **preconfigured hardware systems**.
Potential formats include:
``` id="dist-6"
Raspberry Pi single-node kits
Raspberry Pi cluster kits
prebuilt server appliances
virtual machine images
container bundles
```
These would allow users to run the platform locally with minimal setup.
Implementation details will be determined later.
# Ecosystem Goals
The long-term platform aims to encourage:
``` id="dist-7"
collaborative innovation
distributed compute participation
shared reusable components
rapid product experimentation
```
However, governance, licensing, and commercial structures will be addressed in a future codex revision.
# Temporary Rule
Until governance decisions are finalized:
``` id="dist-8"
focus on architecture
focus on technical capabilities
avoid embedding licensing restrictions into generated systems
```
Agents should not assume any licensing model when generating projects.
# Future Section Placeholder
A future codex revision will define:
``` id="dist-9"
platform licensing
contribution requirements
idea ownership
commercial rights
revenue models
```
This section will eventually become:
``` id="dist-10"
PLATFORM_GOVERNANCE_AND_LICENSE_MODEL
```
### Recommended Next Codex Section
Now that you’ve defined:
- idea scoring
- risk analysis
- ethics agent
- reusable component library
- knowledge system
- distributed nodes
…the **next extremely useful section** would be:
**AGENT ORCHESTRATION ENGINE**
This defines **how agents actually coordinate tasks**, queue work, and collaborate through services like:
-
-
Right now the codex defines *what agents exist*, but not **how they coordinate work**, which is the heart of the system.

- [ ] Dependencies or sequencing constraints are identified.
- [ ] Follow-up engineering tasks are clear enough to execute.

title: AGENT_ORCHESTRATION_ENGINE
summary:
The Agent Orchestration Engine coordinates the execution of tasks across all agents within Dragon Idea Engine.
It ensures that:
``` id="orch-1"
agents receive tasks in the correct order
tasks are distributed efficiently
failures are handled gracefully
workloads are balanced across nodes
agent collaboration is structured
```
Without orchestration, agents would operate independently and produce inconsistent or conflicting outputs.
body:
# Codex: AGENT_ORCHESTRATION_ENGINE

Source section: `codex/sections/11-agent-orchestration-engine.md`

## Summary

The Agent Orchestration Engine coordinates the execution of tasks across all agents within Dragon Idea Engine.
It ensures that:
``` id="orch-1"
agents receive tasks in the correct order
tasks are distributed efficiently
failures are handled gracefully
workloads are balanced across nodes
agent collaboration is structured
```
Without orchestration, agents would operate independently and produce inconsistent or conflicting outputs.

title: AGENT_CAPABILITY_REGISTRY_AND_DISCOVERY
summary:
The Agent Capability Registry maintains a centralized catalog of all available agents, their capabilities, supported technologies, and operational status.
This system allows the platform to dynamically:
``` id="acr-1"
discover agents
route tasks correctly
balance workloads
enable specialization
support distributed nodes
```
Without this registry, the orchestration engine would not know which agents are capable of performing specific tasks.

title: PROJECT_GENERATION_PIPELINE
summary:
The Project Generation Pipeline defines the automated process that transforms an approved idea into a fully structured project, including repositories, architecture, code, documentation, and deployment configuration.
This pipeline ensures that all projects generated by Dragon Idea Engine follow consistent standards and leverage reusable components wherever possible.

This pipeline ensures that all projects generated by Dragon Idea Engine follow consistent standards and leverage reusable components wherever possible.

title: UNITY_DEVELOPMENT_AGENT_SUITE
summary:
The Unity Development Agent Suite enables Dragon Idea Engine to generate **interactive applications, simulations, games, and advanced visual interfaces** using the  engine.
These agents specialize in the unique architecture of Unity projects, including:
``` id="unity-1"
scenes
prefabs
game objects
animation systems
shader pipelines
physics systems
C# gameplay scripts
```
Unity development requires coordination between visual design agents and code agents.

title: ASSET_AND_MEDIA_GENERATION_PIPELINE
summary:
The Asset and Media Generation Pipeline defines the standardized process used by Dragon Idea Engine to generate, validate, store, and reuse media assets required by generated projects.
This system supports:
```text
software UI assets
Unity 2D/3D assets
documentation graphics
training media
hardware diagrams
marketing visuals
```
The goal is to ensure all generated projects have **high-quality, reusable media assets** while minimizing duplication.

title: DISTRIBUTED_AGENT_CLUSTER_ARCHITECTURE
summary:
The Distributed Agent Cluster Architecture defines how Dragon Idea Engine runs across multiple nodes and coordinates agents performing tasks in parallel.
The architecture allows the system to scale from:
```text id="q1w7vn"
single-node Raspberry Pi installation
small Pi clusters
larger distributed compute environments
```
This ensures that idea evaluation, project generation, asset creation, and learning systems can run simultaneously.

title: SECURITY_AND_COMPLIANCE_VALIDATION_SYSTEM
summary:
The Security and Compliance Validation System ensures that every generated project is evaluated for security vulnerabilities, legal compliance, regulatory obligations, and ethical risks before deployment.
This system prevents the platform from generating software, tools, or hardware that could expose users to legal liability, security breaches, or ethical harm.
The system operates as an automated **governance layer** across the entire project generation pipeline.

This system prevents the platform from generating software, tools, or hardware that could expose users to legal liability, security breaches, or ethical harm.
The system operates as an automated **governance layer** across the entire project generation pipeline.

title: AUTONOMOUS_IDEA_DISCOVERY_SYSTEM
summary:
The Autonomous Idea Discovery System enables Dragon Idea Engine to proactively discover, generate, and evaluate new ideas without requiring direct user input.
Instead of relying only on submitted ideas, the system continuously scans multiple information sources to detect:
```text
unsolved problems
emerging technology trends
inefficiencies in existing tools
new business opportunities
scientific and engineering gaps
```
These discoveries are then submitted into the Idea Evaluation Pipeline.
body:

## Summary

The Autonomous Idea Discovery System enables Dragon Idea Engine to proactively discover, generate, and evaluate new ideas without requiring direct user input.
Instead of relying only on submitted ideas, the system continuously scans multiple information sources to detect:
```text
unsolved problems
emerging technology trends
inefficiencies in existing tools
new business opportunities
scientific and engineering gaps
```
These discoveries are then submitted into the Idea Evaluation Pipeline.

title: AGENT_SELF_IMPROVEMENT_AND_EVOLUTION_SYSTEM
summary:
The Agent Self-Improvement and Evolution System allows Dragon Idea Engine to continuously refine the capabilities of its agents by analyzing performance data and automatically improving agent configurations, prompts, strategies, and task coordination.
Instead of remaining static, the system evolves its own internal intelligence layer over time.
The goal is to ensure that agents responsible for idea generation, software development, asset creation, and analysis become progressively more effective.
body:

Source section: `codex/sections/20-agent-self-improvement-and-evolution-system.md`

## Summary

The Agent Self-Improvement and Evolution System allows Dragon Idea Engine to continuously refine the capabilities of its agents by analyzing performance data and automatically improving agent configurations, prompts, strategies, and task coordination.
Instead of remaining static, the system evolves its own internal intelligence layer over time.
The goal is to ensure that agents responsible for idea generation, software development, asset creation, and analysis become progressively more effective.

title: HUMAN_COLLABORATION_AND_OVERRIDE_SYSTEM
summary:
The Human Collaboration and Override System defines how human users interact with Dragon Idea Engine, provide guidance, approve critical decisions, and intervene when necessary.
While the platform is designed for autonomous operation, human oversight ensures that:
```text
high-risk ideas receive review
ethical concerns are addressed
complex design decisions benefit from human expertise
system errors can be corrected
community collaboration is possible
```
This system balances **automation with responsible human control**.
body:

While the platform is designed for autonomous operation, human oversight ensures that:
```text
high-risk ideas receive review
ethical concerns are addressed
complex design decisions benefit from human expertise
system errors can be corrected
community collaboration is possible
```
This system balances **automation with responsible human control**.
